evidence.reference_title: A novel iPSC-based model of ICF syndrome subtype 2 recapitulates the molecular phenotype of ZBTB24 deficiency.

nodes.description: ICF gene defects cause hypomethylation of pericentromeric satellite repeats and juxtacentromeric heterochromatin, especially around chromosomes 1, 16, and sometimes 9.

evidence.reference_title: DNA methylation in disease: Immunodeficiency, Centromeric instability, Facial anomalies syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The hallmark of this syndrome is hypomethylation of pericentromeric satellite repeats, with mutations in four genes: DNMT3B, ZBTB24, CDCA7 and HELLS, being linked to the disease.

Anomalies and rearrangements associated with DNA hypomethylation in the vicinity of the centromeres (the juxtacentromeric heterochromatin) of chromosomes 1 and 16 and sometimes 9, in mitogen-stimulated lymphocytes, is a hallmark of the syndrome.

nodes.description: In at least ICF2, the immune phenotype can extend beyond a B-cell defect to impaired lymphocyte proliferation, increased cell death, and age-progressive combined immunodeficiency.

nodes.description: ZBTB24 zinc-finger dysfunction can reduce transcriptional activation of CDCA7 and other target genes, recapitulating centromeric methylation defects and reducing hematopoietic progenitor output in an ICF2 iPSC model.

evidence.reference_title: A novel iPSC-based model of ICF syndrome subtype 2 recapitulates the molecular phenotype of ZBTB24 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: We validated that the mutated ZBTB24 protein loses its ability to directly activate expression of CDCA7 and other target genes in the patient-derived iPSCs.

experimental_models.description: The iPSC model recapitulates ZBTB24-dependent transcriptional activation failure and downstream centromeric hypomethylation.
experimental_models.name: Patient-derived ICF2 induced pluripotent stem cells
